openmeter/notification/webhook.purpose: Contract layer for the notification webhook delivery subsystem: defines the composed Handler interface (WebhookHandler + MessageHandler + EventTypeHandler), all shared Input/Output types with validation, the typed error taxonomy, and the event-type registry. Implementations live in svix/ and noop/ sub-packages; callers import only this package.

patterns.name: models.Validator on every Input type
patterns.description: Every *Input struct implements models.Validator with a Validate() method that collects field errors into []error, joins with errors.Join, and wraps with NewValidationError. A compile-time assertion `var _ models.Validator = (*XInput)(nil)` must appear adjacent to the type.

patterns.name: Typed error hierarchy with Is* helpers
patterns.description: Every error category (ValidationError, NotFoundError, RetryableError, MessageAlreadyExistsError, UnrecoverableError) is a distinct struct wrapping an inner error, with a New* constructor and Is* predicate using the generic isError[T] helper. Never return raw errors from handler methods.
patterns.example:
func IsValidationError(err error) bool { return isError[ValidationError](err) }
func isError[T error](err error) bool { var t T; return errors.As(err, &t) }

patterns.name: Handler interface composition over flat interface
patterns.description: Handler embeds WebhookHandler + MessageHandler + EventTypeHandler. New capabilities are added to the appropriate sub-interface, not directly to Handler. Both svix/ and noop/ must satisfy all three sub-interfaces.

patterns.name: EventType as a value type registered in NotificationEventTypes
patterns.description: New event types are declared as package-level vars using EventType{Name, Description, GroupName} literals in events.go and appended to NotificationEventTypes. Missing the append means the event type is never registered with Svix at startup.

patterns.name: MaxChannelsPerWebhook constant enforced at validation
patterns.description: The hard channel limit is expressed as MaxChannelsPerWebhook (10) and ErrMaxChannelsPerWebhookExceeded in errors.go. Channel-count checks in implementations must reference these constants, never inline literals.
patterns.example: var ErrMaxChannelsPerWebhookExceeded = fmt.Errorf("maximum number of channels (%d) per webhook exceeded", MaxChannelsPerWebhook)

key_file_guides.role: Defines all Input/Output types, the Webhook and Message domain structs, ExpandParams, and the Handler interface composition. This is the only file callers need to import.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new method to Handler without updating both svix/ and noop/ implementations causes compile failures. Every new Input type needs a Validate() method and a var _ models.Validator compile-time assertion.

key_file_guides.role: Defines the full error taxonomy and the generic isError[T] helper. All error types used by svix/, noop/, and callers originate here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never add domain error types in svix/ or noop/ — they belong here. Svix HTTP errors are mapped to these types via internal.WrapSvixError in the svix/ sub-package, not here.

key_file_guides.role: Registry of all supported notification event types as package-level vars. NotificationEventTypes slice is passed to RegisterEventTypes at startup.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new event type requires: a const block for Name+Description, a var EventType{...} value, and an append to NotificationEventTypes. Missing the append silently skips Svix registration.

anti_patterns: Adding Svix SDK imports or Svix API calls to this package — all Svix logic belongs in webhook/svix/
Returning raw errors from Input.Validate() — always wrap with NewValidationError so HTTP 400 mapping works via GenericErrorEncoder
Defining new error types in svix/ or noop/ sub-packages — all error taxonomy lives in errors.go
Adding a new Input struct without a var _ models.Validator compile-time assertion
Hardcoding the channel limit (10) inline instead of referencing MaxChannelsPerWebhook / ErrMaxChannelsPerWebhookExceeded

decisions.decision: Handler is a composed interface (WebhookHandler + MessageHandler + EventTypeHandler) rather than a flat 10+ method interface
decisions.rationale: Allows callers to depend only on the capability slice they need and lets noop/ and svix/ implementations be verified against each sub-interface independently at compile time.

decisions.decision: Typed error structs with Is* predicates instead of sentinel errors
decisions.rationale: Enables the HTTP encoder chain to pattern-match on error category (ValidationError → 400, NotFoundError → 404, RetryableError → retry logic) without importing Svix types into the HTTP layer.

decisions.decision: events.go holds all EventType definitions as package-level vars, not inside sub-packages
decisions.rationale: Ensures the canonical event-type list is visible to all consumers (notification service, test helpers) without requiring import of the Svix implementation.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new notification event type (e.g. invoice.voided)
